DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Christopher Dolan, Esq. Megan Irish, Esq. 415-421-2800 Dolan Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Katherine Bearman, Esq. 415-554-4256 Office of the city attorney for Defendant CCSF.
Court reporter: Aaron Ellington, CSR #13449, 510-230-7056, ellingtoncsr@gmail.com.

The court announces it received a timely notice to contest on the Demurrer only. The Court adopts the tentative ruling on all the other motions.

ARGUED. Ms. Bearman agrees with the court's tentative ruling. Mr. Dolan sent a timely notice to contest and presents arguments police officer was not in the workplace and clairifies if Demurrer is to CCSF only. Ms. Bearman presents argument on dangerous condition. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer.

Taken Under Submission.***(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Aaron Ellington, CSR #13449, 510-230-7056, ellingtoncsr@gmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order sustaining in part and overruling in part Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the first and fourth causes of action in Plaintiff's complaint. (see scanned order for details)***
